proven.claim: PROVEN: ternary-branch-collapse at absDiff is prevented (CEGAR-refined encoding)
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: ternary-branch-collapse
; LINE: 19
; REASON: The original encoding modeled an unrelated counter/plural scenario (counter_19, output_meaningful) with no connection to the actual absDiff ternary. It unconditionally asserted output_meaningful=0, making SAT trivial and meaningless. The real claim is that both branches of `d < 0 ? -d : d` collapse to the same value. The consequent returns -d (when d<0) and the alternate returns d (when d>=0); these are equal only when d=0, which contradicts d<0, so no real collapse exists.
(declare-const d Int)
(declare-const result_consequent Int)
(declare-const result_alternate Int)
; consequent branch executes when d < 0 and returns -d
(assert (< d 0))
(assert (= result_consequent (- d)))
; alternate branch returns d
(assert (= result_alternate d))
; collapse condition: both branches yield the same value
(assert (= result_consequent result_alternate))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — when d<0, -d=d implies d=0, contradicting d<0
proven.reason: The original encoding modeled an unrelated counter/plural scenario (counter_19, output_meaningful) with no connection to the actual absDiff ternary. It unconditionally asserted output_meaningful=0, making SAT trivial and meaningless. The real claim is that both branches of `d < 0 ? -d : d` collapse to the same value. The consequent returns -d (when d<0) and the alternate returns d (when d>=0); these are equal only when d=0, which contradicts d<0, so no real collapse exists.
proven.judge_note: cegar-flipped: violation became proof after tightening encoding

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: ternary-branch-collapse
; LINE: 19
; VIOLATION: ternary-branch-collapse at absDiff
(declare-const counter_19 Int)
(declare-const branch_taken Int)
(declare-const output_meaningful Int)
(assert (>= counter_19 0))
; branch_taken = 1 when counter == 1 (consequent), 0 otherwise (alternate)
(assert (= branch_taken (ite (= counter_19 1) 1 0)))
; both branches emit empty string — output is never meaningful
(assert (= output_meaningful 0))
; can we reach the plural branch with a non-trivial counter and still get no useful output?
(assert (> counter_19 1))
(check-sat)
